terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: Argus is a private alpha product for educational investing research. These terms describe the current tester experience and do not replace final legal review.

no_investment_advice.title: No investment advice
no_investment_advice.body: Argus provides educational tools that help you structure and review investing ideas. Argus does not provide investment, legal, tax, accounting, or financial advice. You are responsible for your own decisions.

no_brokerage.title: No brokerage or trading relationship
no_brokerage.body: Argus is not a broker, dealer, investment adviser, fiduciary, exchange, custodian, or trading venue. Argus does not hold funds, place orders, execute trades, or connect to brokerage accounts.

historical_simulations.title: Historical simulations are hypothetical
historical_simulations.body: Historical simulations are hypothetical and depend on the assumptions, data availability, asset support, benchmark defaults, and time windows shown in the product. Simulated returns are not guarantees of future performance.

alpha_changes.title: Alpha availability and changes
alpha_changes.body: Argus is in private alpha. Features may change, break, reset, or be removed without notice. We may limit access, suspend accounts, or remove content that creates security, abuse, compliance, or operational risk.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
eligibility.body: You must be old enough to form a binding agreement in your jurisdiction and allowed to use Argus under applicable law. Do not use Argus on behalf of another person or organization unless you are authorized to do so.

acceptable_use.title: Acceptable use
acceptable_use.body: Do not use Argus to break the law, abuse the service, reverse engineer private systems, overload providers, submit sensitive third-party data, or present generated output as professional advice.

third_party_services.title: Third-party services
third_party_services.body: Argus relies on third-party services for hosting, authentication, market data, model access, and chart rendering. TradingView Lightweight Charts is used for chart rendering and attribution, not as an Argus market data source.

account_support.title: Account support
account_support.body_before_email: During private alpha, account deletion and support requests are handled manually. Contact
account_support.body_after_email: or use the in-app account deletion request.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what Argus handles during private alpha so testers know what happens to account data, conversations, feedback, and provider-backed requests.

data_collect.title: Data we collect
data_collect.body: Argus may collect account details, authentication state, conversations, investing ideas you submit, saved product records, feedback, support requests, browser and device basics, usage events, logs, and error diagnostics.

data_use.title: How we use data
data_use.body: We use data to run the product, authenticate users, save and restore conversations, generate and review historical simulations, debug failures, protect the service, respond to support, and improve private alpha quality.

ai_market_data.title: AI and market data providers
ai_market_data.body: Conversation content and prompts may be sent to OpenRouter and model providers so Argus can interpret requests and draft responses. Market data and asset discovery may use providers such as Alpaca and Kraken. Provider availability can affect supported symbols, time windows, and results.

infrastructure.title: Infrastructure and product services
infrastructure.body: Argus uses Supabase for authentication and product persistence, Render for hosting, OpenRouter for model access, and PostHog only if product analytics are intentionally enabled. TradingView Lightweight Charts is used as a charting library with attribution; it is not the source of Argus market data.

access_retention.title: Access and retention
access_retention.body: Founder and administrator access is limited to operating, debugging, securing, and improving the private alpha. We keep data while your account is active, while needed for support or security, or while required by operational records.

your_controls.title: Your controls
your_controls.body_before_email: You can archive or delete conversations in the app. During private alpha, account deletion is handled manually through the in-app request or by contacting

sale_personal_info.title: Sale of personal information
sale_personal_info.body: Argus does not sell personal information during private alpha. If this changes, the policy and product controls must be updated before that behavior ships.

feedback.title: Provide feedback
feedback.rating_placeholder: Tell us more about your experience...
feedback.attach_files_feature: Optional: add a screenshot, sketch, or example.
feedback.consent: I consent to the Argus team processing this feedback and contacting me for follow-up details if necessary.
feedback.consent_feature: I consent to the Argus team using this feature request and contacting me if follow-up would help.

subheading.positive: What worked well in this response?
subheading.negative: What should be improved in this response?
subheading.general: Share feedback about your Argus experience.

feedback.error: Failed to send feedback. Please try again.
feedback.footer_note: App context like this page and timestamp may be included to help us understand this feedback.
feedback.footer_note_conversation: Your current conversation context may be included to help us understand this feedback.

chat.welcome: Welcome to Argus. Tell me an investing idea in plain language and I will help test it with Alpha-safe assumptions.
chat.text_only_input: Argus can only accept text here right now.
chat.disclaimer: Argus can make mistakes. For education only. Not financial advice.
chat.trigger_create_strategy: I want to create a new strategy.
chat.trigger_create_collection: I want to create a new collection.
placeholder_prompts: How has Apple performed against SPY this year so far?
What if I bought Bitcoin this year so far?
Explain a market term in simple words.
What happens if I hold Nvidia over the last 12 months?
Compare Ethereum and Bitcoin over the last 12 months.
Test buying SPY this year so far.
How does buying strong stocks work?
What if I bought $250 of Nvidia every week over the last 12 months?
Try a simple Solana buy-and-hold idea over the last 6 months.
Why do people compare a stock to SPY?

tsla.label: Test Apple vs SPY
tsla.value: Buy and hold AAPL over the last 12 months with SPY as the benchmark.

btc.label: Test Bitcoin (BTC) hold
btc.value: What if I bought Bitcoin this year so far?

dca.label: Test weekly Nvidia buys
dca.value: What if I bought $250 of Nvidia every week over the last 12 months?

example_queries.q1: What if I bought Apple after big drops?
example_queries.q2: What if I bought Bitcoin when it starts rising?
example_queries.q3: What if I bought Tesla every month?

chat.rate_limit_error: You've reached your request limit. Please wait a moment and try again.
chat.error_offline: Argus could not reach the API yet. Start the FastAPI service, then send your idea again.
chat.error_load: Could not load that conversation. Try again.
chat.error_backtest: I couldn't finish that turn. Try once more, or start a fresh chat if this conversation was restored from an older local session.

recovery.interpreter_unavailable: I saved your message, but I could not turn it into a reliable test setup. Please retry in a moment.
recovery.assumption_edit_unapplied: I saved your reply, but I could not safely apply that assumption change, so I left the current idea unchanged. Please retry the change in a moment.
recovery.setup_change_unapplied: I still have the {{setup_phrase}} in this chat, but I could not safely apply that change. Please retry in a moment.
recovery.confirmation_change_unapplied: I still have the {{setup_phrase}} confirmation in this chat, but I could not safely apply that change. {{action_guidance}}
recovery.latest_result_followup_unavailable: I still have the latest result in this chat, but I could not safely answer that follow-up. Please retry in a moment.
recovery.private_alpha_save_unavailable: I cannot move this into Strategies while that surface is off for private alpha, but the completed run is still part of this chat and can be reopened from the conversation or Recents.
recovery.confirmation_action_guidance: The visible confirmation is still ready. Use the card to start the simulation, or use the card controls to change it.
recovery.confirmation_action_missing_context: I do not have an active confirmation to change. Describe the investing idea again and I will prepare a fresh draft.
recovery.confirmation_action_missing_identity: That confirmation action is missing its card identity. Use the latest card action before continuing.
recovery.confirmation_action_stale_card: That confirmation was updated. Use the latest visible card and I will keep the current confirmation intact.
recovery.confirmation_action_stale_payload: That confirmation payload is stale. Use the latest visible card and I will keep the current confirmation intact.
recovery.confirmation_state_lost: I lost the active confirmation state, but your conversation is saved. I can restate the strategy so you can confirm it again.
recovery.confirmation_cancelled: No problem. I will leave that draft unrun.
recovery.clarification_generation_unavailable: I could not phrase the follow-up clearly just now. Your draft is still here; tell me the detail you want to change, or try again in a moment.
recovery.result_refine_missing: I do not have a completed result to refine. Run a strategy first, then use Refine idea from the result card.
recovery.context_macro_recovery: Macro conditions can be useful context for a historical test. Choose a symbol, strategy, or comparison window and I can help frame a supported experiment.
recovery.context_corporate_events_recovery: Corporate events are most useful when tied to a symbol and period. Choose an equity ticker and I can use events like splits or dividends as context around a supported historical test.
recovery.context_market_movers_recovery: A market move can be a useful starting point for an experiment. Choose a symbol or idea and I can turn it into a supported historical test instead of a live feed.
recovery.context_market_movers_seed_recovery: A short-lived movers snapshot can help pick experiment seeds: {{seeds}}. Treat those as symbols to validate, not recommendations or a live ranking. Choose one and I can shape a supported historical test.
recovery.capability_answer_unavailable: I could not phrase that capability answer clearly just now. Tell me the asset, period, or supported rule you want to test, or try again in a moment.
recovery.runtime_failure: Something went wrong. Your conversation is saved. Please try again.
recovery.artifact_action_invalid_state: That action is no longer attached to the current conversation state. Use the latest visible action or tell me what you want to do next.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_stale: That retry belongs to an older failed run. Use the latest retry action or confirm the setup you want to run.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_missing_artifact_i: That retry is missing its failed-run reference. Use the latest retry action or confirm the strategy you want me to run.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_missing_payload: I do not have a failed run payload to retry. Use the visible Run backtest action again, or confirm the strategy you want me to run.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_non_retryable: I still have the failed setup, but rerunning the same payload will hit the same blocker{{blockerSuffix}}. Adjust the rule, asset, or date range and I will keep the idea intact.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_rebuilt_confirmati: I still have that failed setup. I rebuilt the draft so you can review the card and retry when you are ready.
recovery.artifact_action_retry_inactive: That retry is no longer attached to an active failed run. Use the latest retry action or confirm the setup you want to run.
recovery.execution_data_unavailable: The setup is still here, but I could not get {{dataLabel}} for that run right now. Try again, change the dates, or choose a different supported asset.

clarification.period: What date window should I use?
clarification.period_for_asset: What date window should I use for {{symbol}}?
clarification.assumption_for_asset: What assumption should I adjust for {{symbol}}?
clarification.sizing_amount_schedule: How much should each purchase be, and how often should it happen?
clarification.schedule: How often should the purchases happen?
clarification.rule_definition: What entry or exit rule should I test?
clarification.refinement: What would you like to change?
clarification.refinement_for_asset: What would you like to change, compare, or stress-test next for {{assetText}}?
clarification.unsupported_recovery: That rule does not define when to buy or sell on its own. Which supported direction should I use: {{options}}?
clarification.unsupported_recovery_for_asset: That rule does not define when to buy or sell for {{symbol}} on its own. Which supported direction should I use: {{options}}?
clarification.unsupported_recovery_with_raw_value: {{rawValue}} does not define when to buy or sell on its own. Which supported direction should I use: {{options}}?
clarification.unsupported_recovery_with_raw_value_for_: {{rawValue}} does not define when to buy or sell for {{symbol}} on its own. Which supported direction should I use: {{options}}?

coverage_recovery.no_common_data_window: Those assets and the benchmark do not share a usable data window for one trustworthy test. Change the dates, an asset, or the benchmark.
coverage_recovery.insufficient_common_data: The assets and benchmark share too little history for one trustworthy test. Change the dates, an asset, or the benchmark.
coverage_recovery.market_data_unavailable: The shared history is not available for one trustworthy test right now. Change the dates, an asset, or the benchmark.

simplification_options.rsi_threshold: Use a supported RSI threshold rule

options.ask_supported: Ask about an available result fact

chat.result_trust_strip: Historical simulation · No fees/slippage · Not advice
chat.new_chat_ready: New chat ready. What idea should we test?
chat.resume_prompt: Resuming "{{title}}". What would you like to explore next?
chat.empty_history: Your past chats will appear here.
chat.no_recent_activity: No recent activity yet. Your strategies and chats will appear here.
chat.rename_failed: Could not rename this chat. Try again.

backtest_job.queued_body: Argus has the request and will start shortly.
backtest_job.queued_detail: You can leave this chat and come back; progress is saved.
backtest_job.running_body: Argus is calculating the result in the background.
backtest_job.running_detail: You can leave this chat and come back; progress is saved.
backtest_job.succeeded_body: The result is ready. Argus is loading the saved card.
backtest_job.succeeded_detail: The completed result will replace this progress card.
backtest_job.failed_body: The run stopped before Argus could save a result. Adjust the idea if needed and try again.
backtest_job.failed_retryable_body: The run stopped before Argus could save a result. You can retry this idea from the latest confirmation.
backtest_job.failed_detail: The conversation is saved, and this status will stay here.
backtest_job.expired_body: This run did not complete. Start a fresh backtest when you are ready.
backtest_job.expired_detail: Argus will not keep showing a running state for this job.

discovery.loading: Checking symbols, company names, crypto, currency pairs, and indicators.
discovery.empty: Type after @ to search supported assets and indicators.
discovery.no_results: No supported asset or indicator matched "{{query}}". Try a ticker or official company name.
discovery.unavailable_title: Discovery is taking longer than expected
discovery.unavailable: Try the ticker directly, or keep typing. Argus can still reason about your message.

confirmation.period_adjustment: I adjusted the test period to {{period}} because every asset and the benchmark need a shared data window.

assumptions.modeled_costs: Modeled costs: {{fee}} bps fee + {{slippage}} bps slippage

cost_editor.invalid: Enter percentages of 0 or more (slippage up to {{max}}%).
cost_editor.message: Set fees to {{fee}}% and slippage to {{slippage}}% per trade.

strategies.empty_state: Strategies you save from chat will appear here.
strategies.error_load: Could not load strategies. Make sure the API is running.
strategies.unexecuted_hint: Run this strategy to see performance
strategies.run_hint: Run this strategy to see how {{symbols}} would have performed.

suggestions.title: RSI Threshold
suggestions.desc: Buy or sell when RSI reaches chosen levels.
suggestions.prompt: Design a strategy that buys the dip whenever the RSI falls below 30 and sells when it hits 70 for BTC.

suggestions.title: 10% Stop-Loss
suggestions.desc: Protect your capital with an automated exit rule.
suggestions.prompt: I want a strategy that buys any stock in my list and automatically sells if the price drops 10% from my entry.

suggestions.title: Trend Follower
suggestions.desc: Ride the momentum while the price is above the 50-day MA.
suggestions.prompt: Create a strategy that buys whenever the price is above the 50-day moving average on QQQ.

suggestions.title: Monthly DCA
suggestions.desc: Build positions steadily with fixed recurring buys.
suggestions.prompt: Create a simple monthly DCA strategy that buys SPY on the first trading day of each month.

collections.empty_state: Add strategy to collection or save from chat to see them here.
collections.error_load: Could not load collections. Make sure the API is running.

suggestions.title: Tech Giants
suggestions.desc: Group FAANG or top semiconductor companies in one place.
suggestions.prompt: I want to create a collection of the top 5 tech giants like AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA.

suggestions.title: Energy Transition
suggestions.desc: Climate-focused ideas and clean energy plays.
suggestions.prompt: I want to start a collection for energy transition and solar power ideas.

suggestions.title: Dividend Kings
suggestions.desc: Safe, consistent yield plays for a defensive portfolio.
suggestions.prompt: How should I structure a collection for long-term dividend growth stocks?

suggestions.title: Crypto HODL
suggestions.desc: Store your Bitcoin and Ethereum dip-buying logic.
suggestions.prompt: Create a collection for my long-term crypto accumulation strategies.
